title: [FIRRTL] FIRParserAsserts fails to detect print-encoded assertions when there is a subelement access
body:
```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Foo :
    input cond: UInt<1>
    output io : { clock : Clock}

    when cond :
        printf(io.clock, UInt<1>("h0"), "assert: bar")
```
`circt-translate --import-firrtl` generates
```mlir
firrtl.module @Foo(in %cond: !firrtl.uint<1>, out %io: !firrtl.bundle<clock: clock>) {
    %c0_ui1 = firrtl.constant 0 : !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.when %cond  {
      %0 = firrtl.subfield %io(0) : (!firrtl.bundle<clock: clock>) -> !firrtl.clock
      firrtl.printf %0, %c0_ui1, "assert: bar" 
    }
}
```
The last print op must be converted into firrtl.assert by FIRParserAsserts. The bug is that we only pick when + printf pattern here:
https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/e5c4eacd1a9550bc51be65e70ed1f77618df2aa9/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/Import/FIRParserAsserts.cpp#L240-L243

We must accept when + some other ops + printf pattern as well.
